ORAL ANSWERS TO QUESTIONS
Starred Question Nos. 341 to 344
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177): (cid:179)वे(cid:306)न नं. 341, (cid:174)ी क(cid:332)डा िव(cid:309)े(cid:309)र रडे ्डी ।
HERD PRODUCTIVITY AND CATTLE PERFORMANCE
*341. SHRI KONDA VISHWESHWAR REDDY:
Will the Minister of FISHERIES, ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING
be pleased to State:
(a) the details of herd productivity in Telangana, particularly in terms of milk yield
and overall cattle performance;
(b) the steps taken by the Government to improve herd productivity in the State
including any specific schemes or interventions therefor;
(c) whether any reports or studies have been conducted to assess the impact of
modern breeding technologies such as sperm segregation on herd productivity
in Telangana, if so, the details thereof; and
(d) the steps being taken to ensure that farmers in Telangana have access such
data and resources for improving herd productivity?
पंचायती राज म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा म(cid:194)(cid:214)यपालन, पशुपालन और डेयरी म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी राजीव रजं न िसहं उफ(cid:170)
ललन िसहं ):
(क) से (घ ) िववरण सभा पटल पर रख िदया गया है।
िववरण
(क) तेलंगाना म(cid:164) िवशेषकर दूध उ(cid:194)पादन और गोपश ु (cid:255)दश(cid:170)न के संदभ(cid:170) म(cid:164) गोपशुओ ंऔर भ(cid:167)स(cid:332) क(cid:236)
औसत उ(cid:194)पादकता, का (cid:202)यौरा संल(cid:181)न अनुबंध म(cid:164) िदया गया है।

(ख) दूध उ(cid:194)पादकता और दूध उ(cid:194)पादन बढ़ाने म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) और संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) के (cid:255)यास(cid:332) को
अनपु ू(cid:229)रत और संपू(cid:229)रत करने के िलए, भारत सरकार तेलंगाना रा(cid:186)य सिहत पूरे देश म(cid:164) रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय गोकुल
िमशन को लागू कर रही है। यह पहल दशे ी बोवाइन न(cid:214)ल(cid:332) के िवकास और संर(cid:177)ण, बोवाइन आबादी

सिहत इस योजना के तहत उठाए गए कदम िन(cid:204)निलिखत ह(cid:167)-
(i) रा(cid:213)(cid:367)(cid:211)यापी कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म: इस काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान कवरजे को बढ़ाना
और दशे ी बोवाइन न(cid:214)ल(cid:332) सिहत उ(cid:184)च आनवु ंिशक गुणता वाले सांड(cid:332) के वीय(cid:170) का उपयोग करके
िकसान(cid:332) के (cid:300)ार पर गुणव(cid:176)ापूण(cid:170) कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान (एआई) सेवाए ं (cid:255)दान करना है। तेलंगाना रा(cid:213)(cid:367)(cid:211)यापी
कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के तहत भाग ले रहा है। अब तक तेलंगाना रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164) 30 लाख पशओु ंको
कवर िकया गया है, 37 लाख कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान िकए गए ह(cid:167) और 15 लाख िकसान लाभाि(cid:198)वत ह(cid:242)ए ह(cid:167)।
स(cid:179)े स-सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन का उपयोग करके (cid:194)व(cid:229)रत न(cid:214)ल सुधार काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म: इस काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म का
उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य 90% तक क(cid:236) सटीकता के साथ बछिड़य(cid:332) का उ(cid:194)पादन करना है, िजससे न(cid:214)ल सधु ार को
बढ़ावा िमले और िकसान(cid:332) क(cid:236) आय बढ़े। इस काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के तहत देशी न(cid:214)ल(cid:332) के से(cid:179)स-सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन
के उपयोग को सि(cid:248)य (cid:322)प से बढ़ावा िदया जाता है। तेलंगाना ने स(cid:179)े स सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन का उपयोग
करके कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान सेवाए ं (cid:255)दान करने संबंधी काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के तहत 73,048 स(cid:179)े स सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन
खरीदे ह(cid:167)। अब तक 20,091 खुराक(cid:332) का उपयोग िकया गया ह,ै 5,586 गभ(cid:170)धारण ह(cid:242)ए ह (cid:167) और पैदा ह(cid:242)ए
971 बछड़-े बछिड़ य(cid:332) म(cid:164) से 911 बछिड़य(cid:332) का ज(cid:198)म ह(cid:242)आ ह,ै इस (cid:255)कार बछिड़य(cid:332) का िलंगानपु ात
93% से अिधक रहा है।
हाल ही म (cid:164) देशी (cid:322)प से िवकिसत स(cid:179)े स सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन उ(cid:194)पादन तकनीक शु(cid:322) क(cid:236) गई है
और इस तकनीक से स(cid:179)े स सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन क(cid:236) लागत 800 (cid:321)पये से घटकर 250 (cid:321)पये (cid:255)ित खुराक
हो जाएगी। यह तकनीक हमारे िकसान(cid:332) के िलए गेम च(cid:164)जर सािबत होगी (cid:179)य(cid:332)िक से(cid:179)स सॉट(cid:165)ड वीय(cid:170)
उिचत दर(cid:332) पर उपल(cid:202)ध है। देशी से(cid:179)स सॉट(cid:165)ड सीमेन उ(cid:194)पादन तकनीक देश म(cid:164) देशी बछिड़य(cid:332) क(cid:236)
आबादी बढ़ाने म(cid:164) मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170) भूिमका िनभा रही है।

(cid:250)ामीण भारत म(cid:164) बह(cid:242)उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)यीय कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान तकनीिशयन (म(cid:253)ै ी): म(cid:253)ै ी को िकसान(cid:332) के (cid:300)ार
पर गणु व(cid:176)ापूण(cid:170) कृि(cid:253)म गभा(cid:170)धान सेवाए ं देने के िलए (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त और तैयार िकया गए ह(cid:167) और अब तक
देश म(cid:164) 38,736 म(cid:253)ै ी को (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त और तैयार िकए जा चकु े ह(cid:167) , िजसम(cid:164) से तेलंगाना म(cid:164) 571 मै(cid:253)ी को
(cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त और तैयार िकया गया है।
आईवीएफ तकनीक का उपयोग करके (cid:194)व(cid:229)रत न(cid:214)ल सुधार काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म: भारत म(cid:164) पहली बार,
देशी न(cid:214)ल(cid:332) के िवकास और संर(cid:177)ण के िलए बोवाइन आईवीएफ तकनीक को बढ़ावा िदया गया है।
इस काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के तहत िकसान(cid:332) को देशी न(cid:214)ल(cid:332) के िवकास को (cid:255)ो(cid:194)सािहत करने के िलए (cid:255)(cid:194)येक
सिु नि(cid:306)त गभा(cid:170)व(cid:214)था पर 21000 क(cid:236) लागत आती है िजसम(cid:164) से 5,000 (cid:321)पये क(cid:236) (cid:255)ो(cid:194)साहन रािश
पशुपालन एवं डेयरी िवभाग (cid:300)रा (cid:255)दान क(cid:236) जाती है । िवभाग ने देश भर म(cid:164) देशी न(cid:214)ल(cid:332) को बढ़ावा देने
के िलए 22 आईवीएफ लबै (cid:214)थािपत क(cid:236) ह(cid:167), िजनम(cid:164) तेलंगाना म(cid:164) पी.वी. नरिस(cid:204)हा राव तेलंगाना पशु
िचिक(cid:194)सा िव(cid:309)िव(cid:299)ालय म(cid:164) (cid:214)थािपत 1 आईवीएफ लैब भी शािमल है। अब तक 435 (cid:258)ूण तैयार िकए
गए, 350 (cid:258)ूण ह(cid:214)तांत(cid:229)रत िकए गए और 156 बछड़े व बछिड़या ँ पैदा ह(cid:242)ई।
देशी क(cid:208)चर मीिडया का शुभारभं : देश म(cid:164) आईवीएफ तकनीक को और बढ़ावा देने के िलए
इन-िव(cid:367)ो फिट(cid:170)लाइजेशन (आईवीएफ) हेत ु एक देशी क(cid:208)चर मीिडया लॉ(cid:198)च िकया गया है। यह दशे ी
क(cid:208)चर मीिडया महंगे आयाितत मीिडया से स(cid:214)ती दर(cid:332) पर उपल(cid:202)ध ह,ै िजससे आईवीएफ तकनीक
उिचत दर(cid:332) पर उपल(cid:202)ध होगी।
(ii) सीमेन के (cid:198)(cid:254)(cid:332) का स(cid:320)ु ढ़ीकरण: सीमेन उ(cid:194)पादन म(cid:164) मा(cid:253)ा(cid:194)मक और गणु ा(cid:194)मक सधु ार लाने के िलए

DUES UNDER MGNREGS
*343. SHRI BAPI HALDAR:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) the total dues on account of Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment
Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS) as of now, State-wise; and
(b) the details and the total funds of MGNREGS paid during the last three
years and the current year, State-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT;
AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COMMUNICATIONS (DR.
CHANDRA SEKHAR PEMMASANI):
(a) and (b) A statement is laid on the Table of the House.
STATEMENT
(a) Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (Mahatma
Gandhi NREGS) is a demand driven wage employment Scheme. Fund release
to the States/Union Territories is a continuous process and Central Government
25.03.2025 29
is committed for making funds available to States/UTs for the implementation of
the Scheme as per demand for work on the ground.
State/Union Territory (UT)-wise details of pending liabilities for wage,
material and admin component under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS as on
18.03.2025 are given in the enclosed Annexure-I.
(b) State/Union Territory (UT)-wise details of funds released under Mahatma
Gandhi NREGS during the last three financial year and current financial year
2024-25 as on 18.03.2025 are given in the enclosed Annexure -II.

*Release of funds to the State of West Bengal has been stopped since March 9,
2022, under Section 27 of the Act due to non-compliance with Central
Government directives.
SHRI BAPI HALDAR: Sir, as per the reply of the hon. Minister, like many other
schemes, no Central funds have been released to the State Government of
West Bengal under MGNREGA during the last three years. The continued
suspension of funds has resulted in severe consequences, adversely impacting
the livelihood of lakhs of rural people. My question to the hon. Minister is: when
will the Central Government release all the pending dues under the MGNREGA
Scheme to the Government of West Bengal?
DR. CHANDRA SEKHAR PEMMASANI: Sir, this year we have budgeted Rs.
86,000 crore for MGNREGA. Out of this, Rs. 85,000 crore has been released to
all the States… (Interruptions) Give me one minute; I will come to West Bengal.
When it comes to West Bengal, there is a specific situation.
When the Central Government has given money, multiple things went
wrong. One, there was misappropriation of the funds… (Interruptions) Second,
they have split the works and given them to contractors on a nominated basis…
(Interruptions) I will give you all the right information… (Interruptions)
We sent our audit team, and there were a total of 44 works that were
found to be irregular. They have made full recovery in 34 works. Still, ten other
works need to be done, and the financial misappropriation was estimated to be
Rs. 5.37 crore. Out of that, they have recovered Rs. 2.39 crore. Still, some of
these things have to be taken care of. As soon as these things are taken care of,
25.03.2025 33
our hon. Cabinet Minister will be sitting with the Minister of Rural Development
of West Bengal to resolve those issues.

Sir, we all saw how this Scheme proved to be a lifeline during the COVID-
19 period for rural people. My question to the hon. Minister is whether the
Government is considering increasing the number of guaranteed workdays from
100 to 150 and whether the Government is considering increasing the wages
and bringing parity in wages across the States.
DR. CHANDRA SEKHAR PEMMASANI: Sir, there are three questions. The first
question is whether the wages have been increased. Before 2013 and 2014,
there was no proper mechanism to increase the wages. Since 2014, the
Government of India has compiled CPI, based on agricultural labourers
annually, which came out to around six to seven per cent.
If you look at the last five years, cumulatively, we have increased the
wages by 43 per cent. What does it mean? The State of Haryana has the
highest wages of Rs.374. In the States of Kerala and Karnataka, the wages are
close to Rs.350, while the lowest wages are in some of the North-Eastern
States, which are around Rs.234. So, the wages have been increased.
Secondly, the hon. Member has asked about the increase in the budget.
25.03.2025 34
In 2020, at the time of COVID-19, the Budget Estimate was Rs.61,000 crore.
However, because of COVID-19, we had given Rs. 50,000 crore more, making it
altogether Rs.1,10,000 crore. Even if you look at the last two years, Sir, the
Budget Estimate was Rs.73,000 crore, but we have released close to Rs 98,000
crore. So, there is no question of not releasing the money and not increasing the
funds.
ADV. ADOOR PRAKASH: Sir, thank you very much for allowing me to ask a
supplementary question on this floor.
Sir, the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme
in Kerala is witnessing a sharp decline in its workforce. Around 1.86 lakh
workers opted out the Scheme in the past year. Delayed payment and low
wages are the main reasons for distress among the workers. The Parliamentary
Standing Committee on Rural Development has also suggested linking
MGNREGS wages to inflation and increasing the workdays to 150. The workers
in Kerala have not received their wages for the last three months. An amount of
Rs. 811 crore are due for the State of Kerala under the Scheme.
I would like to know whether the Government will release the due amount
to Kerala without delay, and also take measures to address the issues of
workers including wage revision and increasing the workdays. Thank you.
DR. CHANDRA SEKHAR PEMMASANI: Sir, as I mentioned, the wages are
continuously increasing every year based on the CPI of agricultural labour.
Kerala has one of the second highest wages, which is around Rs. 350, next to
Haryana. That is number one.
25.03.2025 35
Secondly, as the hon. Member said, Kerala has already got close to Rs.
3,000 crore this year alone. During the last year, they also got Rs. 3,500 crore.
The release of payments is a continuous process. As I said earlier, we have
estimated a budget of around Rs. 85,000 crore, and we have given all that
money. Whatever is pending will be released within the next few weeks.…
(Interruptions)
SHRIMATI KANIMOZHI KARUNANIDHI: Sir, the NREGA Scheme itself is
demand-driven, and if you delay the payment by 15 days, you are supposed to
pay it with interest to the workers. An amount of Rs. 4,034 crore are pending for
the past five months, including the material dues and the payment also, for
Tamil Nadu workers. The Chief Minister has also written to the Prime Minister in
this regard. We also met the hon. Minister who kindly said that the dues will be
given to us as soon as possible. But we are still waiting.
When are you going to give us these dues? It has already been delayed
for five months. Are you going to give it with interest also?
DR. CHANDRA SEKHAR PEMMASANI: Sir, actually, this Act was passed by
the UPA Government.… (Interruptions) Please listen to the statistics.…
(Interruptions) Let me answer. … (Interruptions)
Sir, if there is a delay in payment after the 15th day, from the 16th day
onwards, interest at the rate of 0.05 per cent will be paid. According to the Act, if
there is a delay, first the State Government will pay the delay charges.
Thereafter, the Central Government will reimburse that money. This is what the
Act said. But, as I said, Tamil Nadu has already received Rs. 7,300 crore. Tamil
25.03.2025 36
Nadu has a population of seven crore whereas Uttar Pradesh has a population
of 20 crore, but Uttar Pradesh draws close to Rs. 10,000 crore whereas Tamil
Nadu draws more than Rs. 10,000 crore.… (Interruptions) So, there is no
question of not giving money. … (Interruptions)

FARMERS QUITTING/LEAVING AGRICULTURE
*344. SHRI ANUP SANJAY DHOTRE:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has conducted a review to assess the number of
farmers who have quit agriculture during each of the last three years in each
State/UT;
(b) if so, the details and the outcome thereof, State/UT-wise;
(c) the main reasons identified in the review for quitting of agriculture by farmers;
(d) whether the Government has suggested that the farmers shift to cash crop
cultivation from traditional crops;
(e) if so, the details thereof along with the other deficiencies identified during the
review; and
(f) the steps taken/proposed to be taken by the Government to popularize
agriculture and ensure that the farmers do not quit agriculture in near future?

WRITTEN ANSWERS TO QUESTIONS
(i) Starred Question Nos. 345-360
IMPLEMENTATION OF DDU-GKY
*345. DR. GUMMA THANUJA RANI:
SHRI C. N. ANNADURAI:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Deen Dayal Upadhyay Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-
GKY) is being implemented in the country, if so, the details thereof, State-
wise including Tamil Nadu;
(b) the details and total number of individuals trained under DDU-GKY during
the last three years, State and UT-wise;
(c) the details and total number of women trained under the said scheme
during the said period, State and UT-wise;
(d) whether the Government has taken any steps to provide employment
opportunities to the trained youth after completion of the programme, if so,
the details thereof, State and UT-wise;
(e) the number of training centres opened and the modes of training provided
to the rural youth under the said yojana including Andhra Pradesh and
Tamil Nadu particularly in Chennai, Coimbatore and Madurai districts and
the details of amount allocated and spent under the said yojana, State and
UT-wise;
25.03.2025 46
(f) whether any special education or skill development project has been
prepared under the said yojana for rural youth in Tamil Nadu, if so, the
details thereof, State and UT-wise; and
(g) whether any decline has been observed in the number of people trained
and appointments given under the said yojana after the pandemic as well
as completion of the training programme, if so, the details thereof and the
corrective steps taken by the Government in this regard?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a), (b), and(d) Deen Dayal Upadhyay-Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
is an ongoing scheme being implemented in the country including the States of
Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. The Yojana is a placement-linked market
driven skill development program for rural poor youth in the age group of 15-35
years. It empowers the rural poor youth with employable skills and facilitates
their participation in regular labour markets, thus providing them with jobs having
regular monthly wages at or above the minimum wages.
Deen Dayal Upadhyay-Grameen Kaushalya Yojana follows a 3-tier
implementation architecture for implementation. The Ministry of Rural
Development is responsible for policy, funding of central share and technical
support, while State Rural Livelihood Missions (SRLMs) / State Skills Missions
(SSMs) are responsible for funding of State share, implementation and
monitoring controls. Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs), which are mostly
private training partners, are responsible for mobilization, training and
25.03.2025 47
placements. State-wise details of candidates trained and placed including in the
State of Tamil Nadu under the Yojana during the last three and current financial
year are given in the enclosed Statement-I.
(c) State-wise details of the women candidates trained under DDU-GKY during
the last three and current financial year are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(e) The training being imparted to the youths under DDU-GKY is based on the
qualification packs approved by National Skills Qualification Committee (NSQC)
of National Council for Vocational Education and Training (NCVET), Ministry of
Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE). The training consists of
theory, practical and on-the job training (OJT) imparted in domain skills,
computer skills, soft skills and English skills.
The details of active training centres in Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu
particularly in Chennai, Coimbatore and Madurai districts are given in the
enclosed Statement-III and the details of funds allocated and funds spent /
released during the last three and current financial year are given in the
enclosed Statement-IV.
(f) No special education or skill development project has been formulated under
DDU-GKY in Tamil Nadu.
(g) After the pandemic there was a decline in the number of people trained and
appointments given under the scheme. However, the following corrective steps
were taken by the Government in this regard:
Project Implementing Agencies were allowed extension of project

implementation period from 3 to 6 years.
25.03.2025 48
Provision for alternative procedure for conducting placement verification

through Kaushal Bharat Mobile App was provided.
Apart from above, Ministry has initiated Captive Employment

Programme under the Scheme for assured placements of the
candidates.
Ministry has also revised the existing guidelines for increased

placement period, batch-mode payment to the implementing agencies,
etc. The revised version of DDUGKY will be implemented with effect
from 1st April, 2025.
For the next Financial Year 2025-26, an allocation of ₹ 750 Crore

(tentatively) has been kept as Central Share and the training target for
approximate 1,49,000 candidates.
After taking corrective steps, the number of trained and placed candidates
have shown an upward move.

कृ िष और िकसान क(cid:208)याण म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा (cid:250)ामीण िवकास म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी िशवराज िसहं चौहान):

अिधसूिचत कर रही ह(cid:167)। दूसरी ओर, आ(cid:328)ं (cid:255)देश, िहमाचल (cid:255)देश, छ(cid:176)ीसगढ़, के रल, राज(cid:214)थान, उ(cid:176)र
(cid:255)देश और उ(cid:176)राखंड क(cid:236) रा(cid:186)य सरकार (cid:164) आरड(cid:202)(cid:208)यूबीसीआईएस के तहत टमाटर क(cid:236) फसल को
अिधसूिचत कर रही ह(cid:167)।
वष(cid:170) 2016 से काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी फसल बीमा योजना (पीएमएफबीवाई) रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/क(cid:164) (cid:254)
शािसत (cid:255)देश(cid:332) (यूटी) के साथ-साथ िकसान(cid:332) के िलए भी (cid:214)वैि(cid:184)छक है। योजना क(cid:236) श(cid:321)ु आत से लेकर
अब तक 27 रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/क(cid:164) (cid:254) शािसत (cid:255)देश(cid:332) ने एक या अिधक कारण(cid:332) से पीएमएफबीवाई को काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत

तब से रा(cid:186)य ने इस योजना को बदं कर िदया है। तथािप, कृिष एवं िकसान क(cid:208)याण िवभाग (cid:300)ारा िकए

रा(cid:186)य सरकार हाल ही म(cid:164) (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी फसल बीमा योजना म(cid:164) पनु ः शािमल ह(cid:242)ई है, इसिलए रा(cid:186)य (cid:300)ारा

के मा(cid:197)यम से िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता योजना (एफएएस) के ज(cid:229)रए, टमाटर सिहत, उनके िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332)

झारखंड के िनया(cid:170)तक भी शािमल ह(cid:167)। इस योजना के िन(cid:204)निलिखत घटक ह(cid:167):
(i) िनया(cid:170)त इ(cid:256)ं ा(cid:214)(cid:367)(cid:179)चर का िवकास: एपेडा कृिष उ(cid:299)ोग(cid:332) के िवकास और म(cid:208)ू य (cid:174)ंखृ ला म(cid:164) कृिष

और (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं कृत खा(cid:299) उ(cid:194)पाद दोन(cid:332) शािमल ह(cid:167)। योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य खराब होने के कारण होने वाले
नुकसान को कम करना और कृिष उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) का गुणव(cid:176)ापूण(cid:170) उ(cid:194)पादन सिु नि(cid:306)त करना है। इसे (cid:255)ा(cid:302)
करने के िलए, यह फसल कटाई के बाद क(cid:236) ह(cid:167)डिलंग सुिवधाए ँ (cid:214)थािपत करना चाहता है। इस घटक

के ले जैसी फसल(cid:332) क(cid:236) ह(cid:167)िडिलंग के िलए के बल िस(cid:214)टम

सामा(cid:198)य इ(cid:256)ं ा(cid:214)(cid:367)(cid:179)चर सिु वधाए ं

MECHANISATION OF AGRICULTURE
* 347 SHRI RAMASAHAYAM RAGHURAM REDDY:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE be
pleased to state:
(a) the steps taken by the Government to ensure mechanisation of agriculture in
small and marginal landholdings under 2 hectares;
(b) the number of custom hiring centres and farm machinery banks across the
country;
(c) the steps taken to ensure the establishment of such centres at the
panchayat and taluka level;
(d) whether the Government intends to undertake a comprehensive study to
assess the current level of farm mechanisation in the country;
(e) whether the Government intends to bring in a policy on the design
standardisation of farm equipment; and
(f) if so, the details thereof?
25.03.2025 58
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) The emphasis of the Government is always to promote farm mechanization
with the aim of increasing the reach of farm mechanization to small and marginal
farmers and to the regions where availability of farm power is low.
The Department of Agriculture andFarmers Welfare (DA&FW) through the
State Governments is implementing a Centrally Sponsored ‘Sub-Mission on
Agricultural Mechanization’ (SMAM) w.e.f. 2014-15. Under SMAM, financial
assistance is provided to the farmers for purchase of agricultural machines and
equipments on individual ownership basis. Financial assistance is also provided
for establishment of Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs) and Village Level Farm
Machinery Banks (FMBs) in order to make available machines and equipments
to the farmers on rental basis as per their requirements. Crop Residue
Management (CRM) Scheme is implemented from 2018-19 primarily to support
the efforts of the Governments of Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh and NCT of
Delhi to address air pollution and to subsidize machinery required for
management of crop residue.
(b) and (c) Under SMAM, financial assistance for establishment of CHCs of the
project cost up to Rs. 250 lakhs is provided @ 40% of the project cost to the
Rural Entrepreneurs (Rural youth and farmer as an entrepreneur), Cooperative
Societies of Farmers, Self Help Groups (SHGs), Registered Farmers Societies,
Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and Panchayats. Financial assistance
for establishments of FMBs of the project cost up to Rs. 30 Lakhs is provided @
25.03.2025 59
80% of the project cost to the Cooperative Societies of Farmers, Registered
Farmer Societies, Self Help Groups, FPOs and Panchayats. The rate of financial
assistance for establishing FMBs in the North Eastern States is @ 95% of the
project cost. Financial assistance @ 80% of the project is also provided under
CRM scheme for establishment of CHCs of crop residue management machines.
Since inception of the SMAM and as on 28.02.2025, the funds amounting
to Rs. 8110.24 Crores has been released to various states. The States have
supplied more than 19.51 lakh machines and equipments to the farmers on
individual ownership basis and more than 52,000 CHCs/Hi-tech Hubs/FMBs
have been established in various States. Under CRM scheme, during the period
from 2018-19 to 2024-25 (as on 28.02.2025), an amount of Rs. 3607.88 Crore
have been released. The States have established more than 41,900 Custom
Hiring Centres (CHCs) of crop residue management machines and more than
3.23 lakh crop residue management machines have been supplied to these
CHCs and individual farmers in the States.
(d) The DA&FW has entrusted a study in July 2024 on ‘Assessment of Status of
Farm Mechanization and Custom Hiring in India’ to the Central Institute of
Agricultural Engineering (CIAE), Bhopal (MP) under Indian Council of Agricultural
Research (ICAR).
(e) and (f) The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has brought out 296 Indian
Standards in agricultural machinery sector to promote quality, safety, reliability
and to protect user interests through certification and testing. The four Farm
Machinery Training andTesting Institutes (FMTTIs) which are sub-ordinate
25.03.2025 60
institutes of DA&FW and 39 other DA&FW designated /approved testing Centers
at ICAR institutes, State Agricultural Universities (SAUs) and State Agricultural
Departments are testing the agricultural machines and equipments as per these
standards. The tested machines by these institutions are only promoted under
the schemes of DA&FW.

(1) दीनदयाल िद(cid:211)यांगजन पनु वा(cid:170)स योजना (डीडीआरएस) – इस योजना के तहत िद(cid:211)यांगजन(cid:332) को

IMPLEMENTATION OF PMGSY IN WEST BENGAL
*349. SHRI RAJU BISTA:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) the present status of implementation of Pradhan Mantri Gramin Sadak
Yojana (PMGSY) since 2019 in West Bengal;
(b) the number of roads sanctioned under PMGSY scheme since 2019 in
Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts under PMGSY scheme; and
(c) the details of projects, roads constructed/under construction and in
progress along with the funds allocated for Darjeeling and Kalimpong
districts under PMGSY, block-wise?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) Under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), a total of 562
roads of 4,237 km length and 26 bridges have been sanctioned since year 2019
to the State of West Bengal. During this period, the State has constructed 1,921
roads of 5,743 Km length and 54 bridges under various interventions/ verticals
of PMGSY, which includes completion of works sanctioned earlier.
(b) Since 2019, a total of 14 roads of 110 km length and 1 bridge have been
sanctioned in Darjeeling district and 9 roads of 66 km length and 2 bridge have
been sanctioned in Kalimpong district under PMGSY.
The Government of India has recently launched phase IV of PMGSY in
September 2024 to provide all-weather connectivity to 25,000 unconnected
habitations of population size 500+ in plains, 250+ in NE andHill Sates/UTs,
25.03.2025 64
special category areas (Tribal Schedule V, Aspirational Districts/Blocks, Desert
areas) and 100+ in Left Wing Extremism Affected (LWE) affected areas, as per
Census 2011. The timeline for completion of PMGSY-IV is March 2029. The
Ministry is coordinating closely with the State Governments and providing the
required assistance for submission of proposals under the scheme.
(c) The block wise details of projects/ roads sanctioned, completed along with
expenditure in respect of Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts under PMGSY,
since 2019, are as under:
Block wise details of projects under PMGSY for Darjeeling district

The Central funds under PMGSY are released to the State Governments
as a single unit. It is the responsibility of the State to release these funds, along
with State share, to district and sub-district level. The release of funds to States/
UTs for implementation of PMGSY is done on the basis of the proposal received
from the State and it depends, inter-alia, on works in hand, execution capacity of
the State, unspent funds available with the State and compliance with
programme guidelines.
Since 2019, an amount of Rs 1,962.81 crore has been released as
Central share to the State of West Bengal under PMGSY and the State has
incurred expenditure of Rs 4,611.19 crore (including state share). The State has
incurred expenditure of ₹ 14 crore in Darjeeling District and ₹ 4 crore
25.03.2025 66
expenditure incurred in Kalimpong district, till date. The timeline for completion
of PMGSY-III works is March, 2025.
PAYMENT OF WAGES UNDER MGNREGS
*350. SHRI V. K. SREEKANDAN:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether it is a fact that there is no statistically significant difference either in
timely payment of wages or payment rejections between the Aadhaar Based
Payment System (ABPS) and the standard bank account based methods
adopted under the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee
Scheme (MGNREGS), if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether it is a fact that many workers of MGNREGS have repeatedly
complained that their wage payments have been misdirected due to the
ABPS while the names of many workers have been deleted because of a
mismatch between details in their job cards and Aadhaar;
(c) whether it is also a fact that the problems with account based payments can
be resolved locally through the block level computer operator whereas
rejections due to ABPS are centralized and harder to resolve, if so, the
details thereof, State-wise; and
(d) whether the Government has any plans to revert back to the previous pattern
of payment and attendance, if so, the details thereof?
25.03.2025 67
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a)To ensure timely payment of wages to the beneficiaries under Mahatma
Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (Mahatma Gandhi
NREGS) and to address the issues arising due to frequent changes in bank
account numbers of the beneficiaries and subsequent non-updation, it was
decided to implement Aadhar Payment Bridge System (APBS). This has been
made mandatory with effect from 1st January 2024. Payment of wages through
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode has had positive impact on the livelihood of
the beneficiaries as it ensures that the payment reaches directly into the
accounts of the intended beneficiaries. Also, the rejection of the wage
transactions has been minimised after making APBS mandatory compared to
the National Automated Clearing House (NACH) payment mode in the category
of reasons such as “Account closed” and “No such account." The details of total
transactions for wage payments andtransactions rejected for both the reasons
since APBS has been made mandatory may be seen below:
Period January, 2024 to February, 2025
SI. No. APBS NACH
1 Total transactions 44,67,50,519 1,63,39,229
2 Rejected 43,789 8,98,829
3 % Rejection 0.0098% 5.50%
(b) to (d) APBS helps in improving the transparency and accountability of wage
distribution under the scheme and ensure faster crediting of wages in the
25.03.2025 68
account of beneficiaries. Aadhar Authentication also reduces leakage and
corruption and ensures that only legitimate beneficiaries with verified identities
receive wages. In case of failure in payment through APBS, an alternative route
to make payment is available through account-based payment that is National
Automated Clearing House. Currently, against total of 13.58 crore active
workers Aadhaar seeding of 99.50% have already been completed. State/UTs
are continuously being monitored by the Central Government for achieving
100% Aadhaar Seeding and APBS conversion in NREGASoft. As and when any
issue is flagged by the State/UT or any other stakeholders, the same is resolved
on priority basis.
Updation/deletion of Job Cards under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS is a
regular exercise conducted by the States/UTs. Job card cannot be deleted for
non-linking of Aadhaar with bank account. To streamline the process for deletion
and addition of Job Cards, Ministry of Rural Development has issued a Standard
Operating Procedure (SOP) vide letter dated 25.01.2025 to all States/UTs, with
clear guidelines regarding deletion and restoration of job cards. The SOP
ensures compliance with the Mahatma Gandhi NREGS guidelines, promotes
transparency and protects the rights of workers by defining conditions for
deletion.
In order to ensure transparency and accountability in the implementation
of Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme, the
Ministry has decided that States/UTs shall ensure capturing of attendance at the
worksite through National Mobile Monitoring System (NMMS) App with two-time
25.03.2025 69
stamped, geo-tagged photographs of the workers in a day for all the works
(except Individual Beneficiary Work) through NMMS w.e.f 1st January, 2023.
Further, in order to avoid any inconvenience to the workers due to NMMS,
it has been decided that in case any worksite is not located in network covered
area or attendance could not be uploaded due to any other network issue then
attendance can be captured in offline mode and can be uploaded once the
device comes into network covered area. In case of exceptional circumstances
owing to which attendance could not be uploaded, the provision for exemption
also exists which has been further decentralized to the level of Block
administration.
The implementation of the NMMS application has significantly improved
the attendance management system and the overall efficiency of the process.
NMMS boosts the credibility of attendance data and fosters trust in the overall
process.
The NMMS application enables the generation of FTOs on the same day,
thereby immensely helping in timely payment of wages.
VILLAGES SELECTED UNDER PMAGY
*351. ADV. GOWAAL KAGADA PADAVI:
SHRI SHASHANK MANI:
Will the Minister of SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT be pleased
to state:
25.03.2025 70
(a) the details of steps taken under the Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana
(PMAGY) to develop villages fulfilling the minimum requirements;
(b) the details and number of villages selected, works carried out and the
number of people benefitted from the said Scheme in the country, State-wise;
(c) the specific monitorable indicators fixed for evaluating any village as 'Adarsh
Gram';
(d) the names of villages included in the list of Adarsh Gram in Dhule and
Nandurbar districts of Maharashtra; and
(e) the details of the schemes being run in the villages selected in Dhule and
Nandurbar districts?
THE MINISTER OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT (DR.
VIRENDRA KUMAR):
(a) Adarsh Gram component of Pradhan Mantri Anusuchit Jaati Abhyuday
Yojana (PM- AJAY) erstwhile scheme of Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana
(PMAGY) is being implemented for integrated development of Scheduled Castes
(SC) majority villages. The selected villages are saturated with identified 50
Socio-Economic developmental indicators as detailed at enclosed Statement-I,
under 10 domains namely Drinking Water and Sanitation, Education, Health and
Nutrition Social Security, Rural Roads and Housing, Electricity and Clean Fuel,
Agricultural Practices, Financial Inclusion, Digitization Livelihood and Skill
Development.
(b) Under Adarsh Gram component of Pradhan Mantri Anusuchit Jaati Abhyuday
Yojana (PM-AJAY) 29,847 villages have been identified. Out of these identified
25.03.2025 71
villages, 11,022 villages have been declared as Adarsh Gram in which 38,883
works have been carried out by the States/UTs benefiting 1,00,53,218
Scheduled Caste population. The State wise list is given at the enclosed
Statement-II.
(c) The list of 50 socio-economic developmental Monitorable Indicators to
evaluate a village as “Adarsh Gram” is given at the enclosed Statement-I.
(d) to (e) No village has been identified under the Adarsh Gram component in
Dhule and Nandurbar districts of Maharashtra.

10.2 % of eligible youth who have been able to avail bank linked loan
10.3 % of households which have at least one member as a member of any SHGs

ONE DISTRICT, ONE PRODUCT AND DISTRICTS AS EXPORT
HUBS INITIATIVE
*352. SHRI G. LAKSHMINARAYANA:
Will the Minister of COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the products identified under the One District, One Product and
Districts as Export Hubs (ODOP-DEH) initiative for Andhra Pradesh along with
the criteria used for their selection;
(b) the details of funds allocated and utilized for capacity building, infrastructure
development and promotional activities under the ODOP-DEH initiative in
Andhra Pradesh, district-wise;
(c) whether any district from Andhra Pradesh has been selected by the Exim
Bank under the Grassroot Initiatives for Development (GRID) program in
collaboration with the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT);
25.03.2025 75
(d) if so, the details of the proposals submitted by Andhra Pradesh for districts
under the GRID program;
(e) the details of proposals approved under the GRID program along with the
funds allocated, released and utilized for the said proposals; and
(f) the details of infrastructure facilities created under the GRID program and
the number of beneficiaries impacted by the program?
THE MINISTER OF COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY (SHRI PIYUSH GOYAL):
(a) The Districts as Export Hubs (DEH) initiative, launched in August 2019, is
aimed at identifying, branding, and promoting products and services from each
district in India that possess unique economic potential. The core objective of the
initiative is to boost exports by leveraging the inherent strengths and competitive
advantages of each district, while also promoting decentralized and inclusive
export growth.
Under the DEH framework, products and services have been identified through a
consultative process with the respective State Governments and Union
Territories, taking into account factors such as existing production clusters,
export potential, local specialization, employment generation, and historical or
cultural significance of the product.
In the case of Andhra Pradesh, a detailed list of products and services identified
under the DEH initiatives, district-wise, is given in the enclosed Statement -I.
One District One Product (ODOP) initiative aims to select, brand, and
promote at least One Product from each District (One District – One Product) of
the country for enabling holistic growth across all regions.
25.03.2025 76
The selection of products for the ODOP is as per recommendations of the
concerned State/UT authorities. Under the ODOP initiative, States/UTs select
the products for the ODOP List by taking into consideration the existing
ecosystem on the ground, potential for exports, and GI-tagged products. The
finalized list is communicated to DPIIT by the relevant Department of
States/UTs.
Details of unique products identified (District-wise) under the ODOP
initiative as on date from the State of Andhra Pradesh is given in the enclosed
Statement-II.
(b)The ODOP-DEH initiative is presently an ongoing initiative which leverages
existing funds under various schemes and does not have separate outlay under
the said initiative. The promotional activities are undertaken with existing funds
of Department of Commerce, DPIIT and the concerned state government.
(c) to (f) As part of the Pilot Project under the Grassroots Initiatives for
Development (GRID) program of Exim Bank, in collaboration with the DGFT, no
district from Andhra Pradesh has been included at the Pilot stage.
STATEMENT-I
District-wise detailed list of products and services identified under the
DEH initiatives in Andhra Pradesh

START-UPS UNDER NTTM
*354. SHRI SURESH KUMAR KASHYAP:
Will the Minister of TEXTILES be pleased to state:
(a) the manner in which the Government supports start-ups in terms of funding,
infrastructure, and policy assistance along with the number of start-ups
registered in Himachal Pradesh;
25.03.2025 86
(b) the manner in which these 4 start-ups align with the National Technical
Textiles Mission (NTTM) and the broader goals of the textile industry;
(c) whether there are any plans to expand this initiative and approve more start-
ups in the near future; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF TEXTILES (SHRI GIRIRAJ SINGH):
(a) The Government has launched “Grant for Research and Entrepreneurship
across Aspiring Innovators in Technical Textiles (GREAT)” scheme under the
National Technical Textiles Mission (NTTM) to support start up ecosystem of
Technical Textile in India. Under the GREAT scheme, a maximum support of
Rs. 50 lakhs is provided to each approved proposal.
Further, till date no start-up applications have been approved to individual/
start-up company based on Himachal Pradesh.
(b) A total of seventeen (17) startup proposals have been approved with a total
cost of Rs. 8.80 Crores till date under the GREAT scheme of NTTM. These
startups are majorly working in the development of Specialty Textiles, Medical
textiles, Textile Composites, High Performance Textiles, Sustainable Textiles,
Protective Textiles which aligns with the National Technical Textiles Mission.
These startups are expected to create an ecosystem for manufacturing of
technical textiles in India and improve the domestic market size and reducing
dependency on imports.
(c) and (d) The applications for start-up proposals can be submitted under the
GREAT scheme which is valid till March 2026.
25.03.2025 87
GROWTH IN FISHERIES SECTOR
*355. SHRIMATI DAGGUBATI PURANDESWARI:
Will the Minister of FISHERIES, ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING
be pleased to state:
(a) the details of factors contributing to the growth of India’s fisheries sector
and its rising contribution of approximately 8% to global fish production;
(b) the details of initiatives, policy interventions and technological
advancements that have facilitated this expansion and improved
productivity in the fisheries industry particularly in Andhra Pradesh;
(c) the significance of sustainable fishing practices and marine resource
management in ensuring long-term sectoral growth while maintaining
ecological balance; and
(d) the steps taken to enhance India’s fisheries exports, strengthen its global
market position and improve competitiveness in international trade?
THE MINISTER OF PANCHAYATI RAJ; AND MINISTER OF FISHERIES,
ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING (SHRI RAJIV RANJAN SINGH ALIAS
LALAN SINGH):
(a) and (b) India’s fisheries sector is experiencing significant growth, achieving a
record production of 184.02 lakh tons and contributing approximately 8% to
global fish production. During last 10 years, the Government of India has
stepped up investments in fisheries and aquaculture sector with a cumulative
investment to the tune of Rs 38,572 crore through various programmes and
initiatives namely Blue Revolution Scheme, Fisheries and Aquaculture
Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF), Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada
25.03.2025 88
Yojana, Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PMMKSSY). The
BR Scheme with its multi-dimensional activities implemented for 5 years from
2015-16 to 2019-20 infused an investment of about Rs. 5000 crore into the
fisheries sector. The FIDF launched in 2018-19 with a fund size of Rs 7,522.48
crore, provides concessional financing for fisheries-related projects. A total of
141 proposals, including fishing harbours, fish landing centers, and processing
units, worth Rs 5,915.54 crore, have been approved under this scheme. The
PMMSY, in operation from 2020-2025 with an estimated investment of Rs
20,050 crore, focuses on bridging critical gaps in production, technology, post-
harvest infrastructure, value chain modernization, fisheries management, and
fisher welfare. To date, Rs 20,990.79 crore worth of projects have been
approved, with a central share of Rs 8,926.29 crore. The Pradhan Mantri Matsya
Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PMMKSSY) aims to formalize the fisheries sector,
incentivize aquaculture insurance, improve value chains for micro and small
enterprises, and ensure safe fish production, with an investment of Rs 6,000
crore. Further, since 2018-19, the government has extended the Kisan Credit
Card (KCC) facility to fishers and fish farmers, sanctioning loan amount of Rs
2,982.58 crore through 4.63 lakh KCCs to support their working capital needs.
The schemes of the Department have emphasized expansion of
aquaculture through adoption of advanced aquaculture technologies such as
biofloc, Recirculatory Aquaculture System (RAS), raceways, and cage culture
units. These interventions have helped improving the productivity and reducing
production costs. In the marine fisheries sector, upgradation of fishing vessels
25.03.2025 89
for export competency, introduction of deep sea fishing vessels and the
establishment of smart and integrated fishing harbors have contributed to
sustaining the production growth.
An investment of Rs 2887.05 crores have been made in Andhra Pradesh
under the Blue Revolution and PMMSY schemes since 2015-16. Under the
PMMSY, the DoF, MoFAH&D, Government of India has approved the proposals
received from the Government of Andhra Pradesh for 39 activities (32
beneficiary components and 7 non-beneficiary components) at a total cost of Rs.
2398.72 crore, with a central share of Rs. 559.10 crore. Under FIDF, ten
infrastructure projects worth Rs 1396.83 crore were approved for the Andhra
Pradesh state. The Government of Andhra Pradesh has informed that the
regulatory framework brought in by the state for development of aquaculture
together with the schematic interventions have helped the development of the
fisheries and aquaculture in the State leading to an increase in fish production
from 20.18 Lakh tonnes in 2013-14 to 51.58 lakh tonnes in 2023-24.
(c) To ensure the long-term growth and ecological balance of Indian fisheries
sector, the Government of India has notified the ‘National Policy on Marine
Fisheries, 2017’ to guide responsible and sustainable marine fishing in the
country. The DoF, GoI, in consultation with scientific institutions advises the
coastal States/UTs to take steps for optimizing fishing efforts and implementing
measures to ensure sustainable fisheries. Destructive practices like paired
bottom trawling and LED light fishing are banned in the Exclusive Economic
Zone (EEZ), and a uniform 61-day seasonal fishing ban is enforced to protect
25.03.2025 90
breeding stocks. Juvenile fishing is also discouraged. Further, under the flagship
scheme of the Department “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana
(PMMSY)”, assistance is provided for activities such as sea ranching,
mariculture, installation of Artificial Reefs and Seaweed Cultivation, etc. for
enhancing the fish stocks, habitat restoration, promoting additional livelihood for
fishers and coastal communities to alleviate fishing pressure in the inshore
areas; and for overall enhancement of marine fish production in a sustainable
manner. Coastal states/UTs manage territorial waters through Marine Fishing
Regulation Acts (MFRAs) including reserving zones for traditional fishers who
are using non-motorized and motorized fishing boats. The status of stocks is
periodically reviewed by the research institutes ICAR-Central Marine Fisheries
Research Institute (CMFRI) and the Fishery Survey of India periodically. As per
the latest report, 91.1% of the 135 fish stocks in different regions are in healthy
and sustainable state.
(d) The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY), a flagship
scheme implemented for overall development of fisheries and aquaculture
sector in the country, inter-alia envisages to enhance fisheries exports to Rs 1.0
lakh crores by 2024-25. In order to enhance India’s export competitiveness and
higher price realization, the PMMSY supports a basket of interventions/activities
along the fisheries value chain including quality fish production, expansion,
diversification and intensification of brackish water aquaculture, promotion of
export-oriented species, infusion of technology, robust disease management
and traceability, training and capacity building, creation of modern postharvest
25.03.2025 91
infrastructure with seamless cold chain, development of modern fishing harbours
and fish landing centres, etc. The seafood exports of India have more than
doubled since FY 2013-14. While the seafood exports stood at Rs 30,213 crore
in 2013-14, the same has increased to Rs. 60,523.89 crore during FY 2023-24.
The Government of India, Ministry of Commerce and Industry has
established the Marine Products Export Development Authority (MPEDA) as a
dedicated agency to facilitate export of seafood. The MPEDA promotes Indian
fisheries products through brand promotion, exporter enrolment, and importer
liaison. It enhances industry capacity via training, awareness campaigns,
workshops, and chintan shivirs. To explore new markets, MPEDA participates in
international trade fairs, organizes Buyer-Seller Meets (BSMs) and Reverse
Buyer Seller Meets (RBSMs), and collaborates with Indian Missions abroad. To
ensure quality meeting international standards, the Export Inspection Council
(EIC), Department of Commerce conducts periodic awareness and training
programs for relevant stakeholders across the value chain. The Government
also conducts bilateral meetings with major importers (U.S., EU, China, Japan)
to boost seafood exports. To strengthen India's seafood sector globally, the
government is facilitating ease of business by amending regulations,
streamlining imports and reducing import duties on key aquafeed ingredients,
aquaculture inputs, and fish processing ingredients.
LIVESTOCK AND FISH DISEASES
*356. SHRI RAJABHAU PARAG PRAKASH WAJE:
25.03.2025 92
Will the Minister of FISHERIES, ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING be
pleased to State:
(a) whether the Ministry has identified major livestock and fish diseases in
Nashik district through surveys during the last five years, and if so, the key
findings regarding disease prevalence, if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has implemented vaccination and disease
prevention programs like the Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) Control
Programme for livestock and any targeted disease management for fisheries, if
so, the details thereof including the details of animals and fish treated;
(c) whether specific funds were allocated for disease control in Nashik, if so,
the details thereof including the status of their utilization;
(d) the steps taken to strengthen veterinary and fisheries healthcare
infrastructure in Nashik's rural and tribal areas for early disease detection and
treatment; and
(e) whether the Government has any plans to introduce advanced technologies
for real-time disease monitoring and control in the region and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF PANCHAYATI RAJ; AND MINISTER OF FISHERIES,
ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING (SHRI RAJIV RANJAN SINGH ALIAS
LALAN SINGH):
(a) Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)-National Institute of
Veterinary Epidemiology and Disease Informatics (NIVEDI) has identified 15
economically important diseases of livestock nationally. As per the information
received from the State of Maharashtra, diseases like Foot and Mouth Disease
25.03.2025 93
(FMD), Peste des Petits Ruminants (PPR), Lumpy Skin Disease (LSD),
Haemorrhagic Septicaemia (HS), Black Quarter (BQ), Enterotoxaemia (ET)
Anaplasmosis, Avian Influenza (AI) and Ranikhet Disease (RD) have been
reported in the district of Nashik during the last five years. As per the information
of Maharashtra, the key findings related to Nashik district are as follows:
i. Last outbreak of AI, Anaplasmosis and PPR occurred during 2020-21. After
that, no outbreak reported.
ii. Last HS and FMD outbreak occurred during 2021-22. After that, no outbreak
reported.
iii. The outbreak of LSD has reduced to 7 in the year 2024-25 from 192 in the
year 2023-24.
Further, as far as fish disease is concerned there is no disease outbreak
reported from the Nashik district during recent years in respect to fish diseases.
(b) The treatment of livestock diseases is under the domain of the State
Government. However, the details of the support being provided centrally for
control programme, surveillance, capacity building etc is as follows:
i. Under Livestock Health and Disease Control Programme (LHDCP) scheme,
100% central assistance is provided to States/UTs for vaccination against Foot
and Mouth Disease (FMD), Brucellosis, Peste des Petits Ruminants (PPR) and
Classical Swine Fever (CSF). Quality vaccines are procured and supplied
centrally. Support is provided to the States/UTs for procurement of vaccination
accessories, strengthening cold chain infrastructure, remuneration for
vaccinators, awareness generation etc.
25.03.2025 94
ii. Under Assistance to States for Control of Animal Disease (ASCAD) sub-
component of the LHDCP scheme, financial assistance is provided to the
States/UTs for the vaccination of state prioritized exotic, emergent and zoonotic
animal diseases including Lumpy Skin Disease, Black Quarter, Haemorrhagic
Septicaemia etc. Assistance is also provided to States/UTs for strengthening of
diagnostic infrastructure and Capacity Building and training of Veterinarians and
Para Veterinarians.
iii. Total 110.49 crore FMD vaccine, 4.57 crore Brucellosis vaccine, 25.36 crore
PPR vaccine, 0.70 crore CSF vaccine, and 27.31 crore LSD vaccine have been
administered to the susceptible animal population in respective states/UTs. In
state of Maharashtra, total 921.26 Lakh FMD vaccine, 28.01 Lakh Brucellosis
vaccine, 227.24 Lakh PPR vaccine, 1.51 Lakh CSF vaccine, and 396.00 lakh
LSD vaccine have been administered in the State.
iv. Ministry through National Surveillance Programme on Aquatic Animal Diseases
(NSPAAD) has developed a mobile app ("Report Fish Disease") which
empowers farmers to report incidence or outbreak. Targeted disease
surveillance is in places for specific pathogen, species and location where there
is occurrence of aquatic animal diseases.
(c) Under LHDCP scheme funds are allocated to State and distribution of funds
to the district is under the domain of the state. Rs.18094.67 lakhs has been
released under the LHDCP Scheme to the state of Maharashtra during last five
years and the utilization certificate details of Rs 1026.32 lakhs is pending from
the State of Maharashtra.
25.03.2025 95
Under Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) fund of Rs.33.78
crore has been approved for a period of three years for the implementation of
the second phase of the National Surveillance Programme for Aquatic Animal
Diseases (NSPAAD) under PMMSY for implementation in all the states/UTs.
Total funds of Rs. 150.09 lakhs have been allocated for the state of Maharashtra
in the last five years under NSPAAD.
(d) As information received from the state of Maharashtra, there are 266
veterinary institutes in Nashik which include 01 district polyclinic, 06 taluka mini-
polyclinic and 109 grade-1 and 148 grade-2 institutes and 2 state mobile units
to cater veterinary care in the district and 04 numbers of MVU has been
operationalized in Nashik under central assistance.
Government has launched an android-based mobile app as “Report Fish
Disease” developed by ICAR-National Bureau of Fish Genetic Resources
(NBFGR), Lucknow under National Surveillance Programme for Aquatic Animal
Diseases (Phase- II), funded under PMMSY. The app is available in Marathi
besides Hindi, English and 10 other regional languages.
(e) The details are as follows:
i. As per the information received from the state of Maharashtra, Western
Regional Disease Diagnostic Laboratory has been upgraded to Bio Security
Level (BSL)-III designed for research and diagnostic work involving potentially
harmful microbes, requiring high containment and bio-safety measures. Two
Regional Disease Investigation Laboratories (RDILs) have been established in
Nashik region with RT-PCR testing facility for disease diagnostic purpose.
25.03.2025 96
ii. Department of Fisheries has developed a mobile app as “Report Fish
Disease” developed by ICAR-(NBFGR), provide a central platform for connecting
and integrating fish farmers, field-level officers and fish health experts
seamlessly with benefits as under:
a. Early Disease Detection and Reporting: fish farmer can report fish
diseases in real-time, enabling early identification and quick response.
b. Rapid Response and Control Measures: Authorities receive immediate
alerts, allowing timely intervention to control outbreaks.
c. Enhanced Aquaculture Biosecurity: It helps prevent disease spread,
reducing fish mortality and economic losses.
d. Scientific Data for Policy Formulation: The collected data aids in research
and policymaking for better disease control strategies.
Besides, two collaborating centres, namely ICAR- Central Institute of
Fisheries Education, Mumbai and College of Fisheries, Ratnagiri have been
designated to investigate disease outbreak from all districts of Maharashtra and
suggest the management measures.

िट(cid:200)पणी: एक िव(cid:176)ीय वष(cid:170) के दौरान आवास िनमा(cid:170)ण म(cid:164) िपछल े िव(cid:176)ीय वष(cid:334) के ल(cid:224)य(cid:332) क(cid:236) तुलना म(cid:164) ह(cid:242)ए िनमा(cid:170)ण

DISBURSAL OF FUNDS UNDER PM-KISAN SCHEME
*358. SHRI BENNY BEHANAN:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE
be pleased to state:
(a) the details of progress in disbursal of funds under the PM-Kisan scheme in
Financial Year 2024, State-wise;
(b) the number of ineligible beneficiaries identified and the amount recovered
from them;
(c) the total number of applications received under the scheme in 2024, State-
wise and the number of applications rejected; and
(d) the timeline for implementation of Aadhaar-based verification for all
beneficiaries under the scheme?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) to (c) The PM-KISAN scheme is a central sector scheme launched in
February 2019 by the Hon’ble Prime Minister to supplement the financial needs
of cultivable land-holding farmers. Under the scheme, a financial benefit of Rs
6,000/- per year is transferred in three equal instalments, into the Aadhaar
seeded bank accounts of farmers through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode.
25.03.2025 100
A farmer-centric digital infrastructure has ensured that the benefits of the
scheme reach all eligible farmers across the country without involvement of any
intermediaries. Maintaining absolute transparency in registering and verifying
beneficiaries, the Government of India has disbursed over Rs 3.68 lakh Cr to
farmers in 19 installments since inception. Under the 19th instalment of the PM-
KISAN Scheme, on 24th February 2025, an amount of more than Rs. 22,000
Crore was released to more than 9.8 Crore farmers. The State-wise details of
funds disbursed during FY 2024-25 is given in the enclosed Statement.
As per operational guidelines of the Scheme, States/UTs are mandated to
identify and verify the eligible beneficiaries under the scheme and upload the
details of eligible farmers on PM-KISAN Portal. Benefits of the scheme are
transferred to the beneficiaries through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode,
based on the verified data received from the States/UTs on the PM-KISAN
portal. The scheme initially started on a trust-based system, where beneficiaries
were registered by the states on self-certification basis. Initially, Aadhaar
seeding was also relaxed for some of the States. Later on, to address this,
several technological interventions were introduced, including integration with
PFMS, UIDAI, and the Income Tax Department. In order to ensure that benefits
are released only to the eligible beneficiaries, land seeding, Aadhaar based
payment and eKYC have been made mandatory. The benefits of the farmers,
who did not complete these mandatory criteria, were stopped. As and when
these farmers complete their mandatory requirements, they will received the
benefits of the scheme along with their due installments, if any.
25.03.2025 101
Further, States/UTs are mandated to recover any amount transferred to
ineligible farmers marked due to higher income groups such as income tax
payees, employees of PSUs, State/Central Govt., Constitutional post holders
etc. An amount of Rs. 416 Cr. has been recovered from the ineligible
beneficiaries so far across the country.
The registration of farmers in the Scheme is an ongoing process. Farmers
can self-register themselves online through PM-Kisan Portal. All such
applications are approved by the concerned States/UTs after due verification. In
the cases, where the required documents/details are not provided by the
applicant, the application is liable to be rejected by the State/UT Governments.
Once it is approved by the State/UTs, benefit is processed immediately by the
Department and the same is released in the subsequent instalment.
(d) Aadhaar is one of the key parameters for establishing identity of the farmers
in scheme and the benefits of the scheme are transferred to only Aadhaar
authenticated farmers. At present, 100% PM KISAN beneficiary’s database is
Aadhaar seeded, authenticated and e-KYC verified. The disbursement of the
benefits of the scheme are released through Aadhaar Based Payment System
i.e. into Aadhaar seeded bank account of beneficiaries. From the 15th
Installment cycle of the scheme (Aug. 2023 – Nov. 2023), Aadhaar based e-KYC
has also been made mandatory under the scheme.
25.03.2025 102

SAANSAD ADARSH GRAM YOJANA
*359. DR. PRABHA MALLIKARJUN:
SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has achieved the goals, objectives set under
Saansad Adarsh Gram Yojana (SAGY) in the State of Karnataka
particularly in Davangere Lok Sabha Constituency, if so, the details
thereof;
(b) the details of Gram Panchayats selected and transformed as model
villages under SAGY in the said constituency along with the details of
infrastructure created under the yojana since the inception of the scheme;
(c) the details of funds sanctioned and released for implementation of SAGY
to the State of Karnataka particularly to Davangere Lok Sabha
Constituency;
25.03.2025 104
(d) the number of villages in the country which have been adopted and
developed as model villages under SAGY during the last five years, State
and year-wise;
(e) whether this scheme has failed and none of the villages could be
developed as model village during the last eleven years; and
(f) if so, the details thereof and the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) and (b) The primary goal of Saansad Adarsh Gram Yojana (SAGY) was the
holistic development of three Adarsh Grams by March, 2019 and thereafter, five
such Adarsh Grams (one per year) till 2024 through the leadership and guidance
of Hon’ble Members of Parliament (MPs). Under SAGY, the components of an
Adarsh Gram are context specific. However, under the development activities, it
includes Personal development; Human development; Social development;
Economic development; Environmental Development; Social Security; Good
Governance; and Basic amenities and service.
Under SAGY, a total of 134 gram panchayats were selected by the
Hon’ble MPs in the state of Karnataka under which 14,041 planned
projects/activities have been completed. In Davanagere district, Hon’ble MP has
selected a total of 6 Gram Panchayats namely Avaragola, Holesiriger,
Kandagallu, Mallapura, Mustur and Suraturu. In these Gram Panchayats 692
planned projects/activities have been completed and the details are given in the
enclosed Statement -I.
25.03.2025 105
(c) SAGY is a 100% convergence based scheme without allocation of any funds
for developmental projects undertaken under the scheme. Resources for SAGY
projects are mobilized from the funds available under Central andState
schemes, CSR funds and Member of Parliament Local Area Development
Scheme (MPLADS) fund andMember of Legislative Assembly Development
Scheme (MLALAD) Fund.
(d) In the past 5 years, 830 Gram Panchayats across the country have
been developed under SAGY Scheme by completing 100% projects/activities
planned under the Village Development Plan. Year-wise and state-wise details
are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(e) and (f) No, till date 1737 Gram Panchayats across the country have been
developed under the Sansad Adarsh Gram Yojana by completing 100% of the
projects/activities planned under the Village Development Plan.
STATEMENT-I
Details of the Gram Panchayats where planned projects/activities have
been completed

(ii)Unstarred Question Nos. 3911 to 4140
AMNESTY SCHEME FOR CHANDIGARH HOUSING BOARD ALLOTTEES
3911. SHRI MANISH TEWARI:
Will the Minister of HOME AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the total notices issued to residential and commercial properties by the
Chandigarh Housing Board (CHB) from June 1, 2014, to March 1, 2025, along
with actions taken, including properties resumed, residents evicted, fines levied,
and criminal cases filed or compounded year-wise;
(b) total revenue earned by CHB through fines and penalties on allottees during
the period 1st June 2014 to 1st March 2025;
25.03.2025 174
(c) total houses re-allotted to original allottees or to other people between June
1, 2014, and March 1, 2025, after payment of fines;
(d) whether the Union Government plans to introduce an amnesty scheme or
enact legislation, similar to the 1999 Delhi scheme or the NCT of Delhi Laws
(Special Provisions) Second (Amendment) Act, 2023, to provide relief to
Chandigarh allottees, given the problems caused by the unceasing flow of
notices from the CHB; and
(e) if so, the details thereof and if not, reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS (SHRI
NITYANAND RAI):
(a) to (c) The total notices issued to residential and commercial properties by
the Chandigarh Housing Board (CHB) from June 1, 2014, to March 1, 2025 and
action taken thereon are given in the enclosed Statement. Total revenue earned
by CHB through fines and penalties on allottees during this period is ₹3.72
crores. Further, 35 houses were re-allotted to original allottees during the same
period.
(d) and (e) There is no such proposal. The Chandigarh Master Plan 2031
provides for systematic examination of structural safety of the housing units and
strict enforcement of Building Byelaws in Chandigarh. Further, Hon'ble Supreme
Court of India in its judgment dated 10.01.2023 in the SLP (C) No. 4950 of 2022
has directed to freeze Floor Area Ratio.
25.03.2025 175

2025 म(cid:164) काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन के िलए इंजीिनय(cid:229)रंग िदशािनद(cid:165)श और (cid:198)यूनतम (cid:255)णाली मानक(cid:332) का नवीनतम
स(cid:214)ं करण (cid:255)कािशत िकया है। इन तकनीक(cid:236) मानक(cid:332) का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य खेत से उपभो(cid:265)ाओ ंतक िनबा(cid:170)ध एडं -
टू-एडं कनेि(cid:179)टिवटी सिु नि(cid:306)त करना ह,ै िजसम(cid:164) स(cid:214)टेनेिबिलटी को बढ़ाने और फसल-उपरांत हािन
को कम करने के िलए अ(cid:177)य ऊजा(cid:170)-आधा(cid:229)रत (cid:255)णािलय(cid:332) को एक(cid:236)कृत करने पर िवशेष (cid:197)यान िदया
गया है।
इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:265), रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय बागवानी बोड(cid:170) (एनएचबी) "बागवानी उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) के िलए को(cid:208)ड (cid:214)टोरेज
और भंडारण के िनमा(cid:170)ण/िव(cid:214)तार/आधुिनक(cid:236)करण के िलए पूंजी िनवेश सि(cid:202)सडी" नामक एक योजना
को काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रहा है। इस (cid:214)क(cid:236)म के तहत, 5000 मीि(cid:367)क टन से अिधक और 20000 मीि(cid:367)क
टन तक क(cid:236) (cid:177)मता के को(cid:208)ड (cid:214)टोरेज और िनयंि(cid:253)त वातावरण (सीए) भंडारण के
िनमा(cid:170)ण/िव(cid:214)तार/आधुिनक(cid:236)करण के िलए सामा(cid:198)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) प(cid:229)रयोजना क(cid:236) पूंजी लागत के 35% क(cid:236) दर
से और पूव(cid:333)(cid:176)र, पहाड़ी और अनसु ूिचत (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) के मामले म(cid:164) 50% क(cid:236) दर से (cid:248)े िडट िल(cid:179)ं ड बैक-एडं ेड
सि(cid:202)सडी उपल(cid:202)ध है।

(क) सरकार (cid:300)ारा राज(cid:214)थान के पारंप(cid:229)रक हथकरघा और ह(cid:214)तिश(cid:208)प उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) को घरले ू और

ह(cid:214)तिश(cid:208)प उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) क(cid:236) माक(cid:165) िटंग, बनु कर म(cid:254)ु ा ऋण आिद के िलए िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता (cid:255)दान क(cid:236) जाती

 व(cid:318) म(cid:253)ं ालय भौगोिलक संके तक (जीआई) अिधिनयम, 1999 के तहत पारंप(cid:229)रक िडजाइन(cid:332)
और पटै न(cid:334) को सरु (cid:177)ा (cid:255)दान करने क(cid:236) भी मांग कर रहा है। यह म(cid:253)ं ालय जीआई अिधिनयम के
तहत िडजाइन(cid:332)/उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) को पंजीकृत करने और जाग(cid:322)कता पैदा करने के िलए सेिमनार,
काय(cid:170)शालाए ं आिद आयोिजत करने के िलए िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता (cid:255)दान करता है।
 पारपं (cid:229)रक हथकरघा िडजाइन(cid:332) को संरि(cid:177)त करने और हथकरघा (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) िडजाइन-उ(cid:198)मुख
उ(cid:194)कृ(cid:310)ता का िनमा(cid:170)ण करने के िलए जयपुर, राज(cid:214)थान सिहत देश भर म(cid:164) 16 बनु कर सेवा
क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) म(cid:164) िडजाइन संसाधन क(cid:164) (cid:254) (cid:214)थािपत िकए गए ह(cid:167)।
 हथकरघा एवं ह(cid:214)तिश(cid:208)प एज(cid:164)िसय(cid:332)/बनु कर(cid:332)/कारीगर(cid:332) को अपने उ(cid:194)पाद बेचने के िलए माक(cid:165) िटंग
ए(cid:179)सपो/इव(cid:164)ट्स का आयोजन करके माक(cid:165) िटंग (cid:200)लेटफाम(cid:170) (cid:255)दान िकया जाता है। िपछले तीन
वष(cid:334) के दौरान राज(cid:214)थान रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164) कुल 43 ए(cid:179)सपो/इव(cid:164)ट्स आयोिजत िकए गए ह(cid:167)।
 उ(cid:194)पादकता, माक(cid:165) िटंग (cid:177)मताओ ं को बढ़ाने और बेहतर आय सिु नि(cid:306)त करने के िलए
राज(cid:214)थान रा(cid:186)य क(cid:236) 16 उ(cid:194)पादक कं पिनय(cid:332) (पीसी) सिहत देश भर के िविभ(cid:198)न रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) म (cid:164) 380
से अिधक उ(cid:194)पादक कं पिनयां (पीसी) बनाई गई ह(cid:167)।
 बनु कर(cid:332) और कारीगर(cid:332) को सरकारी ई-माक(cid:165) ट (जेम) पर ऑन-बोड(cid:170) करने संबधं ी कदम उठाए
गए ह(cid:167) तािक वे अपने उ(cid:194)पाद सीधे िविभ(cid:198)न सरकारी िवभाग(cid:332) और संगठन(cid:332) को बेच सक(cid:164) । अब
तक लगभग 1.80 लाख बनु कर जेम पोट(cid:170)ल पर ऑन-बोड(cid:170) िकए जा चुके ह(cid:167)।
(ख) िनिधय(cid:332) का आवंटन रा(cid:186)य-वार नह(cid:233) िकया जाता है। रा(cid:186)य सरकार(cid:332) और अ(cid:198)य हथकरघा
संगठन(cid:332) से (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:255)ाि(cid:302) के आधार पर िनिधयां जारी क(cid:236) जाती है।
(ग) और (घ) हथकरघा और ह(cid:214)तिश(cid:208)प म(cid:180)ु य (cid:322)प से (cid:211)यि(cid:265) आधा(cid:229)रत हाउसहो(cid:208)ड गितिविध है िजस
पर (cid:198)यूनतम मजदरू ी अिधिनयम लागू नह(cid:233) होता है। य(cid:299)िप, जैसा िक ऊपर बताया गया है, योजनाब(cid:294)
इंटरव(cid:164)श(cid:198)स के मा(cid:197)यम स,े व(cid:318) म(cid:253)ं ालय देश भर के बुनकर(cid:332) और कारीगर(cid:332) को (cid:211)यवसाय िवकास और
आय सजृ न के पया(cid:170)(cid:302) अवसर (cid:255)दान करते ह(cid:242)ए डायर(cid:179)े ट माक(cid:165) िटंग (cid:200)लेटफॉम(cid:170) (cid:255)दान करता है।
25.03.2025 185
MICRO IRRIGATION SUPPORT TO FARMERS
3915. SHRI GURMEET SINGH MEET HAYER:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE be
pleased to state:
(a) the steps taken by the Government to promote micro-irrigation in Punjab’s
Malwa region to address groundwater depletion as the region, including districts
of Sangrur and Malerkotla, faces severe groundwater depletion and Micro-
irrigation (drip and sprinkler systems) can help conserve water and improve
efficiency, especially for vegetable and horticulture farming;
(b) whether there are any subsidies or financial incentives for farmers to adopt
drip and sprinkler irrigation, particularly for vegetables and horticulture;
(c) the details of the current coverage of micro-irrigation in the region and the
details of the future targets; and
(d) whether there are any specific initiatives for Sangrur and Malerkotla,
considering its significant vegetable and horticulture production and
high water demand, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF AGRICULTURE AND
FARMERS WELFARE (SHRI RAM NATH THAKUR):
(a) Per Drop More Crop (PDMC) Scheme is under implementation in the country
including State of Punjab since 2015-16. PDMC focuses on enhancing water
use efficiency at farm level through Micro Irrigation, namely, drip and sprinkler
irrigation systems. The scheme is being implemented in all districts of Punjab
including Malwa region.
25.03.2025 186
(b) Under PDMC Scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers for
installation of micro irrigation systems @ 55% and 45% of the unit cost to small
and marginal farmers and other farmers respectively in various crops including
vegetables and horticulture crops. Apart from this, the State Government of
Punjab is providing additional top-up subsidy to encourage farmers to adopt
micro irrigation.
(c) As reported by State Government of Punjab, since 2015-16 till February,
2025, 26,104 hectares has been covered under Micro-irrigation through PDMC
scheme in Malwa region.
(d)As reported by State Government of Punjab, in order to meet high water
demand for cultivation of various crops including vegetable and horticulture
crops in Sangrur and Malerkotla districts, State Government of Punjab is
providing financial and technical assistance for laying of underground pipeline
from canal outlets for irrigation of tail-end and far-flung areas.
MICRO-IRRIGATION FACILITIES IN GUJARAT
3916. SHRI DHAVAL LAXMANBHAI PATEL:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE be
pleased to state:
(a) the measures that have been implemented to improve irrigation facilities in
Gujarat under the 'Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana' (PMKSY);
25.03.2025 187
(b) the area of agricultural land brought under micro-irrigation systems through
PMKSY in valsad;
(c) the details of increase in crop yield and farmer in valsad income attributed to
enhanced irrigation infrastructure; and
(d) the details of financial assistance provided to farmers in valsad for adopting
water-efficient irrigation technologies?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF AGRICULTURE AND
FARMERS WELFARE (SHRI RAM NATH THAKUR):
(a)Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) is implemented by the
Department of Water Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation
(DoWR, RDandGR, M/o Jal Shakti) and the Department of Land Resources
(DoLR), M/o Rural Development. Presently PMKSY has three components,
namely, Accelerated Irrigation Benefit Programme (AIBP); Har Khet Ko Pani
(HKKP) and Watershed Development Component (WDC 2.0). The following
projects/schemes are being implemented in the State of Gujarat under PMKSY
to improve irrigation facilities in the state.
i) Sardar Sarovar Project, along with pari-passu implementation of
Command Area Development and Water Management under AIBP
being implemented by DoWR, RD and GR.
ii) 33 nos. of schemes under Repair, Renovation and Restoration (RRR) of
Water Bodies under HKKP being implemented by DoWR, RD and GR.
iii) 51 nos of watershed projects under WDC-PMKSY 2.0 being implemented
by DoLR.
25.03.2025 188
(b) and (c) Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Per Drop More Crop (PDMC)
Scheme is being implemented since 2015-16 to increase water use efficiency
through micro-irrigation systems, namely drip and sprinkler. From 2015-16 to
2021-22, the Scheme was implemented as a component of PMKSY. From 2022-
23, the scheme is being implemented under Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Krishi
Vikas Yojana (PM-RKVY). As reported by the State Government an area of 6831
hectares has been covered under micro irrigation in Valsad District of Gujarat
from 2015-16 till date. As per Study report on PDMC carried out by State
Government, crop yield increased up to 25-30% and farmers income increased
up to Rs. 15487/ha in all the districts of the State including Valsad district due to
adoption of micro irrigation.
(d)Under PDMC Scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers for
installation of micro irrigation systems @ 55% and 45% of the unit cost to small
and marginal farmers and other farmers respectively. The State Government of
Gujarat is also providing additional top-up subsidy to encourage farmers to
adopt micro irrigation including those in Valsad district. As reported by State
Government an amount of Rs. 23.37 crore have been provided as financial
assistance to farmers at Valsad district for adoption of micro-irrigation from
2015-16 to 2024-25.
